skill.name: video-edit
skill.description: Edit existing video on RunComfy — this skill is a smart router that matches the user's intent to the right edit model in the RunComfy catalog. Picks Wan 2.7 Edit-Video (general restyle / background swap / packaging swap, identity + motion preservation), Kling 2.6 Pro Motion Control (transfer precise motion from a reference video to a target character), or Lucy Edit Restyle (lightweight identity-stable restyle / outfit swap). Bundles each model's documented prompting patterns so the skill gets sharper edits without burning iterations on the wrong model. Calls `runcomfy run <vendor>/<model>/<endpoint>` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "video edit", "edit video", "restyle video", "swap video background", "motion control", "outfit swap video", or any explicit ask to transform a video.
skill.summary: Routes video editing tasks to the optimal AI model on RunComfy based on user intent.
skill.author: doany-ai

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found 101 potential issues, but all are false positives or legitimate usage patterns. The 66 external command detections are code examples in markdown showing runcomfy CLI usage. The 14 network detections are legitimate URLs to RunComfy and GitHub. The 12 high-severity findings (weak crypto, path traversal) have no evidence in the actual file content. The skill is a legitimate video editing model router that invokes the RunComfy CLI with structured JSON input, with documented security controls including no shell injection surface and whitelisted API endpoints.

medium_findings.title: External Command Execution via RunComfy CLI
medium_findings.description: The skill invokes 'runcomfy run' commands to process video editing tasks. Commands pass structured JSON arguments with no shell expansion, limiting injection risk. This is the intended behavior of the skill.

low_findings.title: Network Access to External API Endpoints
low_findings.description: The skill references URLs to RunComfy API and documentation. All endpoints are legitimate RunComfy services (runcomfy.com, runcomfy.net). No evidence of data exfiltration or malicious callbacks.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Write Operations
low_findings.description: The skill writes generated video files to a user-specified output directory. The CLI enforces a 2 GiB download cap. No evidence of unauthorized file access or path traversal.

content.user_title: Edit Videos with AI Model Routing on RunComfy
content.value_statement: Choosing the wrong AI video model wastes time and produces poor results. This skill reads the user intent and selects the best model from the RunComfy catalog with correct prompting patterns.
actual_capabilities: Selects the best video editing model based on user intent
Generates CLI commands for Wan 2.7 Edit-Video, Kling Motion Control, and Lucy Edit Restyle
Preserves identity and motion in edited videos with correct prompting patterns
Handles background swaps, outfit changes, packaging swaps, and cinematic restyles
Transfers motion from a reference video to a target character
Supports audio preservation and resolution configuration
limitations: Cannot edit videos directly - requires the RunComfy CLI to be installed
Only works with models in the RunComfy catalog
Each call uses one model - no multi-model blending available
Source video constraints apply: 2-10 seconds, 100 MB max for Wan route

use_cases.title: Restyle Talking Head Videos
use_cases.description: A content creator wants to change the background of a recording without affecting the speaker's face or lip movement.

use_cases.title: Swap Product Packaging in Video
use_cases.description: A product marketer needs to replace packaging in a video using a reference image while keeping original hand motion and framing.

use_cases.title: Transfer Motion Between Videos
use_cases.description: A motion designer wants to map the dance motion from a reference clip onto a different character shown in a still image.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Video Restyle
prompt_templates.prompt: Edit this video by changing its style: [describe the new look]. Keep the subject's face and movements the same.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user with a simple restyle request

prompt_templates.title: Background Swap with Audio
prompt_templates.prompt: Swap the background of this video to [new background description]. Preserve the speaker face, pose, and lip movement. Keep the original audio.

prompt_templates.title: Motion Transfer from Reference
prompt_templates.prompt: Transfer the motion from this reference video to the character shown in this image. Target character is [description]. Motion reference shows [motion type].
prompt_templates.scenario: Animator mapping motion from one video to another character

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Reference-Conditioned Packaging Swap
prompt_templates.prompt: Replace the product packaging in this video using the provided reference image. Maintain original hand movement and camera motion. Use Wan 2.7 Edit-Video with reference_image input.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user with specific model knowledge and reference assets

output_examples.input: Restyle my talking head video with a warm cinematic color grade. Keep my face and lip movements exactly the same.
output_examples.output: Selected Wan 2.7 Edit-Video for general restyle. Generated CLI command with preservation-first prompt, audio setting set to origin to keep source audio.

output_examples.input: Make this character dance like the person in the reference video.
output_examples.output: Selected Kling 2.6 Pro Motion Control for motion mapping. Generated CLI command with target character image and motion reference video, character orientation set to image for 10-second output.

output_examples.input: Change the outfit in this video to a business suit. Keep the face and motion exactly the same.
output_examples.output: Selected Lucy Edit Restyle for localized outfit swap. Generated CLI command with identity preservation prompt, resolution defaulting to 720p.

best_practices: Start prompts with preservation goals before describing the change to keep identity and motion intact.
Use one edit direction per call. Compound edits reduce motion quality and introduce visual drift.
Provide source videos within model constraints. Wan Edit requires 2-10 seconds at 100 MB max.
anti_patterns: Combining multiple edit types in one call. Each model handles one edit direction best.
Using reference images for general restyle tasks. Only use reference_image for direct design transfer.
Requesting full character swaps with Lucy Edit Restyle. It is built for localized style changes, not full subject replacement.

faq.question: What is the RunComfy CLI and do I need it?
faq.answer: The RunComfy CLI is a command-line tool that submits video jobs to the RunComfy API. You need to install it with 'npm i -g @runcomfy/cli' and log in with an account.

faq.question: What video formats and sizes are supported?
faq.answer: Supported formats are MP4, MOV, WEBM, and GIF. Limits depend on the model: Wan Edit supports 2-10 seconds at up to 1080p with a 100 MB cap.

faq.question: How does the skill decide which model to use?
faq.answer: The skill reads a routing table that matches user intent to the best model. Restyle goes to Wan Edit, motion transfer goes to Kling, and lightweight outfit swaps go to Lucy Edit.

faq.question: Can I use video files from my computer?
faq.answer: The RunComfy API needs a URL to access the video. You can upload your video to a hosting service and pass the URL. The CLI does not upload local files directly.

faq.question: What happens to my uploaded videos and data?
faq.answer: Video URLs are sent to RunComfy model servers over HTTPS. The skill only connects to whitelisted RunComfy endpoints. No telemetry or callbacks are sent.

faq.question: How long does video processing take?
faq.answer: Processing time depends on the model, video length, and server load. Wan Edit and Kling typically process within a few minutes for short clips.
